note: Created directly in doing (user: create card + move straight to doing). Connect crawls fake 99% bar on a created-but-not-running Pod (sc2vdkrk6cvb3s, RTX 2000 Ada) because the flow has no RunPod runtime-status signal — only /remote/comfy/status. MPI-86 watchdog already fired correctly; this fixes the misleading bar via a getPod-status poll.
Implementation started. maturity idea->in-progress, status active. Add backend getPod-status route + poll it in _pollEngineReady to detect EXITED/not-running and stop the fake progress.

Code complete. Backend /remote/comfy/status now attaches podStatus (throttled getPod desiredStatus) when wrapper /health not ready; renderer _pollEngineReady bails on EXITED/TERMINATED/DEAD past 30s grace, deletes the dead Pod via delete-active (stops EXITED container-disk billing), resets bar to 0, shows host-failure hint. node --check + require + ESLint clean. Healthy slow-boot + MPI-86 watchdog untouched. validation.md written — phantom-Pod repro is USER-only.
maturity in-progress->validating (validation state exists, awaiting USER live phantom-Pod verify).

USER live-verified on hardware: env-stub forced not-running on boot auto-connect -> bar did NOT crawl to 99%, status flipped to stopped ~30s, yellow host-failure hint, Connect live, Pod deleted from RunPod console (both UI + console screenshots match). Stub removed (grep-clean). Regression: plain npm start, real L4 reconnect booted to ready normally (no false-trip). Second path (js/shell.js boot auto-connect) had the same bug, fixed + verified mid-test. Accepted.
